name: Apfelkuchen (Apple Cake)
directions: Preheat 400
Whisk drys in bowl
Work cold butter into drys; Will have a crumb texture
Whisk mik and egg together; Add to drys; Will be thick and sticky
Spoon batter into greased 9 in spring form or loaf pan
Spread apples across the top; They will dry as it bakes so use more than less
Bake 20 min
Whisk 1/4 of cream with 2 tsp flour until smooth; Add other custard ingredients; Combine
When done first bake; Remove and pour custard over the top
Reduce heat to 350 and bake 30-35 minutes or until golden brown
Cool in pan a few minutes then on wire rack

name: Banana Pistachio Cake
directions: Cream butter and sugar
Add eggs, mashed banana, vanilla
Add drys to creamed
Add milk
Fold in nuts
Pour batter into 2 pans
Bake at 350 for 45-55 min

name: Chai Gingerbread Cupcakes
directions: Sift flour, ½ C sugar, brown sugar, baking powder, spices, S&P
Make a well and add oil, buttermilk, molasses and vanilla
Beat for 1 min; Add egg yolks; Beat 1 min
Whip egg whites in seperate bowl until foamy
Add ½ C sugar and beak until stiff peaks
Fold whites into batter
Spoon into muffin cups and bake at 375 for 15-18 min

name: Chocolate Wacky Cake (Vegan)
directions: Sift drys in a bowl
Mix wets in another bowl
Mix wet into drys; Do not overmix
Pour into 2 10in cake pans (greased and floured)
Bake at 325 for ~25 min

name: Flourless Chocolate Torte
directions: Melt chocolate, butter, cream, ½ C sugar
Whisk egg yolks with the vanilla
Loosly whisk eggs whites with ½ C sugar
Slowly temper chocolate into egg yolks/vanilla
Fold chocolate/yolk mix into egg whites/sugar
Mix until incorporated
Pour into spring form lined with parchment
Bake at 350 for 90 min with a water bath; Rotate every 30 min

name: Gingerbread Cake
directions: Mix flour, respresso, cocoa, powdered ginger, baking powder, salt and pepper
Cream butter and sugar
Add eggs one at a time to the sugar and butter
Add fresh ginger to the creamed butter mixture
Add molasses to the creamed mixture
Fold in the drys
Bake at 350 for 50-60 minutes

name: Lavender Ice Cream
directions: Gently heat milk and lavender (do not boil) ~5 min
Strain out lavendar
Stir in salt and sugar
Stir in vanilla and cream
Chill immediately, minimum 2 hours
Put in ice cream machine
If no ice cream machine: place in stainless steel bowl in freezer and whisk every 15 min until thickened

name: Lemon Crumble Loaf Cake
directions: Oven 325; Grease a 9x5 (2L) loaf pan; Parchment on bottom
Mix all dries for crumble; Add melted butter until mixture is crumbly (will be soft)
Sift flour, sugar, baking powder, salt
Add butter and lemon and beat with paddle on low until butter is incorporated
Whisk sour cream, eggs, yolk, vanilla; Add to flour mixture on low; Then mix medium until smooth (30 sec)
Stir in berries if using
Scrape into pan, level top, sprinkle with crumble mix
Bake 60-70 minutes until skewer comes out clean; Cool 20 min then turn out onto wire rack

name: Sachertorte
directions: Melt chocolate gently; let cool until just warm
Cream butter, icing sugar, vanilla until pale and fluffy; add yolks slowly
Add melted chocolate to yolk mixture
Whip egg whites with punch of salt to soft peaks; slowly add sugar to stiff peak
Fold a third of whites into chocolate batter; then alternate folding in flour and egg whites to chocolate
Butter and flour 9 in springform
Bake 356 (180 C) for 1 hour
Invert onto a rack; cool completely; turn right side up after 25 min
Heat jam with spalsh of water or rum
Cut cake in half; spread jam; put together; brush sides and top with more jam
Sugar and water in saucepan; bring to boil while stirring; simmer few min; cool
Add chocoloate when syrup is just warm and keep stirring to melt (too hot=dull; too cold=viscous)
Pour over cake and spread on top and sides

name: Sangria Cake
directions: Mix flour, baking powder, salt
Cream butter, sugar and orange zest until pale and fluffy (~3 min)
Reduce speed and add eggs one at a time; Add vanilla
Alternate adding dry mixture and sangria
Add red food colouring if desired
Bake at 350 for 30 min
Bring sugar and water to a boil, reduce heat and simmer for 1 min
Allow to cool completely
Put egg white and sugar and mix until smooth
Put over a double boiler until no longer grainy (~3 min); I don't do this
Use paddle attachment and add in cubed butter slowly and mix until smooth
Add vanilla and colouring
Place a layer of cake on a plate, Brush with simple syrup then top with butter cream; Add berries if desired; Repeat

name: Soft Chocolate Chip Cookies
directions: Cream butter and sugar
Add eggs and vanilla
Stir in drys
Add chocolate chips
Bake for 8-10 minutes at 350

name: Soft Pumpkin Cookies
directions: Combine flour, baking soda, baking powder, cinnamon, nutmeg and salt
Beat sugar and ½ C butter to cream
Beat in pumpkin, egg and vanilla
Slowly beat in dry mixture
Drop by rounded tablespoon onto baking sheet
Bake at 350 for 15-18 min (edges are firm)
Cool on tray 2 min then move to rack
